target.title: [codex] Use frog-green background for Sessions
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Sessions view used the same neutral page canvas as the rest of the Control UI, instead of the requested frog-green background.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Add a route-specific `content--sessions` marker.
- Use a saturated frog green (`#58a63c`) with an ink-dark foreground (`#081108`).
- Preserve the existing card and table surfaces so session data remains easy to scan.
- Cover the route marker with a focused render regression test.

## User Impact

The Sessions page now has a clearly frog-green canvas without changing any session behavior. Its page heading retains a 6.34:1 contrast ratio.

## Evidence

- `pnpm format:check ui/src/styles/layout.css`
- AWS Crabbox `cbx_fc2dc9150f68`, run `run_5c355884895d`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` — 21 tests passed.
- Visible WebVNC browser proof at 1920×1080 with the mock Sessions UI loaded at `/sessions`.
- Final Codex autoreview: no accepted/actionable findings.
